---
title: "Making_autodock_controls_boxplots"
format: html
---

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)

library(here)

#making a reproducible filepath 
project_dir <- here(
  "Docking",
  "AutoDockVina",
  "acyl_tail_lengths",
  "autodock_pdbqt_outputs",
  "controls_docking_outputs"
)

pdbqt_files <- list.files(project_dir, pattern = "\\.pdbqt$", full.names = TRUE)

#check it lists 20 files (10 outputs for positive and 10 outputs for negative control)
length(pdbqt_files)

# Function to extract binding affinities from a single pdbqt file
extract_affinities <- function(file_path) {
  lines <- readLines(file_path)
  # grep lines starting with REMARK VINA RESULT
  vina_lines <- grep("^REMARK VINA RESULT:", lines, value = TRUE)
  # Extract the first numeric value (binding affinity) from each line
  affinities <- as.numeric(sapply(strsplit(vina_lines, "\\s+"), function(x) x[4]))
  return(affinities)
}

# Loop through all files and store results
results <- lapply(pdbqt_files, function(f) {
  affinities <- extract_affinities(f)
  tibble(
    file = basename(f),
    control = ifelse(grepl("negative_control", f), "Negative", "Positive"),
    model = 1:length(affinities),
    affinity = affinities
  )
})

# Combine all results into one dataframe
df <- bind_rows(results)

# Preview
print(df)

# Boxplot of binding affinities
ggplot(df, aes(x = control, y = affinity, fill = control)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Binding Affinity of Models",
    x = "Control Type",
    y = "Binding Affinity (kcal/mol)"
  )
```

```{r}
t.test(affinity ~ control, data = df)

	

wilcox.test(affinity ~ control, data = df)

#chat said both show as stat sig phewwwwwwwwwwwww
```
